title: diteilis elata
intro_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", diteilis elata, which is likely a misspelling and referring to Dioscorea elata, a plant in the Dioscoreaceae family, has been used traditionally to treat various conditions including diabetes.
side_effects: Diteilis elata can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, headaches, and dizziness due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's volatile oils may lead to respiratory problems, including bronchitis and asthma attacks. Additionally, improper use of diteilis elata may cause allergic reactions, such as skin rashes, itching, and hives in some individuals. In severe cases, excessive consumption of the plant's extracts can cause liver and kidney damage, requiring immediate medical attention.
